slides.title: Kissflow Apps & Portals
slides.subtitle: Build custom enterprise applications and external-facing portals
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com > User guide > Apps, Portal

slides.title: What are Kissflow Apps?
bullets: Apps combine pages, navigation, processes, dataforms, boards, and reports
Built with visual page editor and optional JavaScript scripting
Role-based navigation — different menus for different user groups
Publish to specific users, groups, or entire organization
Apps appear on user home page based on access permissions
Citizen developers build apps; IT governs through app admin roles
slides.notes: Apps are the container — everything else lives inside them.

slides.title: App Builder Interface
bullets: Left panel: Pages, Navigation, Processes, Dataforms, Boards, Reports, Settings
Center: page canvas with drag-and-drop component placement
Right panel: component properties and page settings
Component library: views, charts, text, images, buttons, embeds
Preview mode to test app before publishing
Version control with draft and publish workflow
slides.notes: Demo the app builder — show component palette.

slides.title: App Pages and Navigation
bullets: Create multiple pages per app — dashboard, list, detail, settings
Navigation menu — configure links, icons, and ordering
Role-based navigation — show different menus to different roles
Page parameters — pass data between pages dynamically
Breadcrumbs and page titles for user orientation
Default landing page configurable per role
slides.notes: Show navigation configuration for admin vs user roles.

slides.title: Page Components
bullets: Process components — initiate and list process items
Dataform views — data table, gallery, sheet components
Board components — kanban board embedded on page
Report/chart components — analytics visualizations
Text, image, button, divider — layout and UI elements
Custom page components — JavaScript/HTML for advanced UI
slides.notes: Each component binds to a specific flow, dataform, board, or report.

slides.title: AI Custom Page Components
bullets: Describe what you want to build — AI generates page components
Natural language to functional UI with data bindings
Iterate by refining the description or editing generated code
Works within enterprise constraints for predictable output
Announced March 2026 — Product announcement
Accelerates prototyping for citizen developers
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com — Build custom page components with AI

slides.title: App Roles and Permissions
bullets: App Admin — full build and configuration access
Define custom roles within the app (Manager, User, Viewer, etc.)
Map roles to account user groups
Page-level visibility — restrict pages to specific roles
Component-level permissions within pages
Process, dataform, and board permissions inherit from app roles
slides.notes: Role design is critical — plan before building pages.

slides.title: What is a Portal?
bullets: External-facing application interface for non-Kissflow users
Partners, vendors, customers access via dedicated portal URL
Enterprise plan feature — not available on Basic plan
Custom branding: logo, colors, and domain configuration
Separate authentication for external users
Expose selected processes, forms, and data securely

slides.title: Portal Configuration
bullets: Configure portal URL, branding, and welcome page
Define external user registration and login methods
Select which app pages and processes are portal-accessible
Field-level security for external vs internal data visibility
Portal users see only their own submissions and permitted data
Audit log tracks all external user actions
slides.notes: Demo vendor portal or partner onboarding portal if available.

slides.title: App Publishing and Lifecycle
bullets: Draft mode — build and test without affecting live users
Publish — make app available to configured user groups
Unpublish — remove from user home pages without deleting
Archive — retire apps no longer in use
Duplicate apps as starting point for new solutions
Monitor usage via App Monitoring governance module
slides.notes: Emphasize draft/publish discipline for production apps.

slides.title: Apps Best Practices
bullets: Plan information architecture before building pages
One app per business domain — avoid mega-apps
Reuse processes and dataforms across multiple app pages
Design for mobile — test on phone and tablet screen sizes
Use role-based navigation to simplify user experience
Document app purpose and owner for long-term maintainability
